---
title: "CMP registry"
---

{{< include ../_includes/sprfmo-banner.qmd >}}

<div class="paper-title">Candidate Management Procedure Registry</div>

## Status vocabulary

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Test candidate | Defined sufficiently for testing; not shortlisted or adopted. |
| Advanced candidate | Selected in the current analytical workflow for robustness testing; formal status not yet confirmed. |
| Shortlisted | Retained by the working group for formal comparative evaluation. |
| Recommended | Supported by a documented working-group recommendation. |
| Adopted | Formally adopted through the applicable SPRFMO process. |
| Retired | Preserved for traceability but no longer under active consideration. |

The primary registry is derived from the merged `jmMSE` `main` branch.
`model_robustness.R` selects `tun29` and `tun32` for robustness evaluation, and
the 25 July 2026 presentation describes MP29 as a potentially good candidate
and MP32 as the optimal powerramp run. They are **advanced candidates**, not
adopted procedures.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-cmp-registry
#| tbl-cap: "Current advanced candidates derived from jmMSE. Tuned trigger values are those reported in the 25 July 2026 presentation."
#| echo: false

registry <- read.csv("../data/cmp-registry.csv", check.names = FALSE)
knitr::kable(registry)
```

## Candidate pages

- [MP29](mp29.qmd): empirical all-index estimator plus hockeystick HCR.
- [MP32](mp32.qmd): empirical all-index estimator plus power-ramp HCR.

## Development lineage

The `jmMSE` tuning sequence tested targets, minimum catches, limit values, HCR
families, and estimator variants.

| Run | Role in development | July presentation interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| MP16 | Hockeystick, target 2000, no minimum/limit | Potential candidate before minimum/limit refinement |
| MP25 | Powerramp, target 1500, no minimum/limit | Potential candidate before minimum/limit refinement |
| MP29 | Hockeystick with minimum 270 and limit 0.1 | Optimal hockeystick run; advanced to robustness tests |
| MP32 | Powerramp with minimum 270 and limit 0.1 | Optimal powerramp run; advanced to robustness tests |
| MP35-40 | CPUE-only, shortcut-assessment, and perfect-information variants | Estimator sensitivity and diagnostic comparisons |
| MP41-42 | 500-iteration versions of the MP29/MP32 designs | Replication-count sensitivity, not distinct operational CMPs |

The earlier SCW17/Paper-09 rows are retained in source history as workshop test
proposals. They are not the primary candidate list for this site.

## Interpretation safeguards

- `min`, `trigger`, `target`, and `initial` are catch quantities in the current
  code and presentation; final normative units must be stated before adoption.
- `dlow` and `dupp` are interannual lower and upper multipliers where supplied.
- Index names match `model_tune.R` exactly.
- The trigger was tuned to a 0.60 probability for `SB0green` over 2041-2050 on
  the reference setup. It is part of the candidate version and should not be
  silently retuned during annual application.

## Promotion requirement

A candidate can move beyond test status only when it has a completed
[specification](specification-template.qmd), reproducible performance evidence,
an implementation test using frozen inputs, and a decision record identifying
the approving body and date.
